text.title: Diagram-as-Code with Mermaid / Structurizr Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Diagram-as-Code with Mermaid / Structurizr methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Every diagram MUST have a `.mmd` / `.dsl` source file committed; rendered PNG/SVG outputs MAY be committed but the text source is authoritative.
Diagrams MUST render in CI on every PR that touches the source; render failures block merge.
rule.rationale: if it does not render, it is broken.

rule.statement: Markdown docs that reference a diagram MUST link to the source file path (`diagrams/auth-flow.mmd`), not to an embedded PNG.
rule.rationale: Linkable sources survive renames; embeds rot silently.

rule.statement: Structurizr diagrams declare which C4 level they represent (System Context / Container / Component / Code). Mixed-level diagrams are forbidden.
rule.rationale: mixing levels makes the diagram unusable for any reader.

rule.statement: PRs that change a flow MUST update the corresponding diagram source in the same commit. Drift-only PRs are a separate cleanup track.
Rendered outputs in docs MUST carry alt text describing what the diagram shows. Empty alt is rejected.
rule.rationale: Accessibility + diagrams broken in dark-mode / colour-blind contexts.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Diagram-as-Code with Mermaid / Structurizr under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
